data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernization plan:

**Legacy patterns found:**

| # | Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---------|-----------|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | Replace with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports | Handled automatically by SDK |
| 3 | `Configuration`, `Platform` defaults | SDK defaults |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Not needed (defaults) |
| 5 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup`s (OutputPath, DebugType, etc.) | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 6 | Explicit `<Reference>` for framework assemblies | Implicit in SDK-style |
| 7 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` |
| 8 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Move metadata to `<PropertyGroup>`; SDK auto-generates attributes |
| 9 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |

Applying now:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize them.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style project format** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** — SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** — SDK-style handles this automatically
4. **Configuration/Platform property groups** with Debug/Release defaults — SDK-style provides sensible defaults
5. **ProjectGuid** — not needed in SDK-style


6. **FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, Deterministic** — all have sensible defaults in SDK-style
7. **Debug/Release property groups** with DebugSymbols, DebugType, Optimize, OutputPath, DefineConstants, ErrorReport, WarningLevel — all have sensible defaults in SDK-style
8. **Explicit framework assembly references** (System, System.Core, etc.) — implicit in SDK-style
9. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style uses globbing by default
10. **AssemblyInfo.cs** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes; metadata can go in the .csproj
11. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** — should become `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` (or consider upgrading to .NET 6/8)

Now I'm creating a modernized SDK-style project file that consolidates the assembly metadata into the csproj and removes all the legacy boilerplate...

Writing modernized csproj...

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
toolArgs.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines → 11 lines. Here's what changed:

- **Project root** → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` (replaces imports, targets, XML namespace)
- **Target framework** → `net472` (SDK-style short name)
- **Removed**: `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `OutputType` (all defaults)
- **Removed**: `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` (match project filename — default)
- **Removed**: Debug/Release property groups (SDK defaults handle these)
- **Removed**: All `<Reference>` items (framework assemblies are implicit)
- **Removed**: All `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`)
- **Removed**: `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`); SDK auto-generates assembly attributes
